methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.0B - $1.8B.

data.narrative:
Axiom Space, a Series C space infrastructure company founded by Michael T. Suffredini and Kam Ghaffarian, has raised $850M+ to a current post-money valuation of $2.5B. The valuation assessment draws on a comp universe of 5 public-market Space & Aerospace names (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private-stage peers, with 4 same-stage private comparables used as primary anchors.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 1.7x Series C step-up multiplier to the $2.5B base, implying $4.2B forward value. Peer-median triangulation across four same-sector, same-stage companies—Impulse Space ($1.8B), Varda Space Industries ($1.5B), Skyryse ($1.1B), and Isar Aerospace ($1.0B)—yields a median of $1.3B, with a range of $1.0B to $1.8B. Public-comp anchoring is not applicable; although the Space & Aerospace sector median trades at 59.8x price-to-sales across five public names, the target's revenue is not disclosed. The triangulated range spans $1.1B (low) to $4.9B (high), with a median of $2.8B. Current valuation sits near the midpoint. Key uncertainty centers on whether the target will sustain series-stage premium multiples or compress toward peer-median levels; stage-progression assumes meaningful value creation ahead.
